rationale: The cited paper is used in the methods/results setup as prior work that measures banner advertising effects on actual purchasing, not just clicks or attitudes. The citing paper explicitly says its proportional hazard approach is in line with earlier work on banner ads and purchasing, naming Manchanda et al. (2006). This gives the citation a concrete methodological and empirical positioning role rather than a purely passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing study draws on Manchanda et al. (2006) as prior precedent for estimating purchase effects of online banner ads and uses that line of work to justify its own purchase-timing/hazard modeling of retargeting effectiveness.
